content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

October 7, 1987

David Tucker
1715 Donner Ave. #1
Davis, CA 95616

Re: Our File No. G-87-251

Dear Mr. Tucker:

In response to your questions concerning the Political
Reform Act of 1974, I have enclosed the following materials:

1. Three reports summarizing the activities and
accomplishments of the Fair Political Practices Commission from
1974 to 1984.

2. A pamphlet entitled "A Guide to the Political Reform
Act of 1974--California's Conflict of Interest Law for Public
Officials."

3. A pamphlet entitled "A Guide to Government Code Section
84308 of the Political Reform Act of 1974--Conflicts of
Interest and Campaign Contributions."

These materials should assist you in developing the answers
to your questions. Also enclosed is a list of other
publications prepared by the Commission which you may order
from this office.

Sincerely,

[illegible]
Kathryn E. Donovan
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:jaj

Enclosures

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Fair Political Practice commission
Ms. Betty Brown
428 J Street,Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, Ca. 95804


Dear Ms. Brown

My name is David Tucker and I am currently doing a research paper
on the Political Reform Act of 1974 and how has it faired thirteen
years later. This paper is a part of my senior project at Calif.
State Polytechnic University San Luis Obispo.


Below is a list of five questions that I feel are critical to this paper
and I am asking for your assistance in helping me locate the proper
sources or for your input to help make this a quality paper.

The issue that I am covering is the conflict-of-interest part of the
Political reform Act of 1974. I would like to know the following;

1) Was the original intent of the act achieved and to what degree?

2) Has the act helped or hindered the political stability on the local
and state level? Are people interested in politics feeling intimidated
by the harsh rules and regulations that go with entering the political
arena?

3) Is the act still a viable solution for politics in the 80's?

4)Are the enforcement procedures adequately administered on the state
and local level?

5) Is there a possibility of new legislation to clarify the intent
of the Political Reform Act of 1974?

As a political science major I am concerned about the impact of many
laws and regulations that where implemented in the 60's and 70's, and
if the reasons then are still valid today.

Your help in this research will be greatly appreciated. I hope to hear
from you soon.

                                                        Sincerely,

Please send any replies to                             David Tucker
1715 Donner ave. #1
Davis, Ca. 95616

sections.question: 1) Was the original intent of the act achieved and to what degree? 2) Has the act helped or hindered the political stability on the local and state level? Are people interested in politics feeling intimidated by the harsh rules and regulations that go with entering the political arena? 3) Is the act still a viable solution for politics in the 80's? 4) Are the enforcement procedures adequately administered on the state and local level? 5) Is there a possibility of new legislation to clarify the intent of the Political Reform Act of 1974?
sections.question_synthetic: A student researcher seeks information and assistance regarding five critical questions about the effectiveness and impact of the Political Reform Act of 1974's conflict-of-interest provisions, including whether the original intent was achieved, effects on political participation, current viability, enforcement adequacy, and potential for clarifying legislation.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming request from David Tucker and FPPC response letter. The response (from Kathryn E. Donovan) provides materials rather than specific legal advice or conclusions, so classified as incoming_request with response included. No formal CONCLUSION section in response letter.

embedding.summary: This document contains a student's incoming request to the FPPC asking for research assistance on the Political Reform Act of 1974 and its conflict-of-interest provisions for a senior project paper, along with the FPPC's response providing reference materials and publications.
